channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: New research may lead to hearing aids with the ability to select one voice among many
item.description: Scientists have developed an artificial hearing system that harnesses the brain's ability to amplify certain sounds while suppressing others. It could lead to better hearing aids.
item.content:encoded: Scientists have developed an artificial hearing system that harnesses the brain's ability to amplify certain sounds while suppressing others. It could lead to better hearing aids.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: Why the U.S. government is pouring millions into a Montana mining company
item.description: President Trump's visit to China highlights the lock China has on critical and strategic minerals. A big federal investment in Montana mining aims to turn that tide.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's visit to China highlights the lock China has on critical and strategic minerals. A big federal investment in Montana mining aims to turn that tide.
item.dc:creator: Victoria Traxler

item.title: Forest therapy: Why a physician wants more doctors to train in nature-based medicine
item.description: A Boston hospital fights staff burnout and boosts patient morale with forest bathing — getting people outside for some time in nature.
item.content:encoded: A Boston hospital fights staff burnout and boosts patient morale with forest bathing — getting people outside for some time in nature.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bebinger

item.title: Author Anne Lamott shares how she had to unlearn what others thought of her
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author Anne Lamott opens up about how she had to unlearn what others thought of her.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Author Anne Lamott opens up about how she had to unlearn what others thought of her.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin

item.title: Baseball bards from across the country gather to celebrate the sport with poetry
item.description: America's national pastime is celebrated at a minor league park in Massachusetts... with poetry
item.content:encoded: America's national pastime is celebrated at a minor league park in Massachusetts... with poetry
item.dc:creator: Andrea Shea

item.title: Growing number of homeless people caught up in NYC crackdown on sleeping on subways
item.description: A growing number of homeless New Yorkers are finding themselves in court after police caught them taking up more than one seat on public transit or lying on the floor of a station in recent months.
item.content:encoded: A growing number of homeless New Yorkers are finding themselves in court after police caught them taking up more than one seat on public transit or lying on the floor of a station in recent months.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Max

item.title: Opposition leader Maria Corina Machado talks about her plans to return to Venezuela
item.description: <>NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado.
item.content:encoded: <>NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Venezuelan opposition leader Maria Corina Machado.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra Marquez Janse

item.title: Oilfield theft in Texas grows more organized — and more dangerous
item.description: The state of Texas is working to crack down on the growing problem of oilfield theft. Estimated losses from stolen crude across the state total a billion dollars.
item.content:encoded: The state of Texas is working to crack down on the growing problem of oilfield theft. Estimated losses from stolen crude across the state total a billion dollars.
item.dc:creator: David Martin Davies

item.title: Trade and Iran war are on the agenda for Trump's state visit to China
item.description: President Trump meets with China's President Xi Jinping in Beijing with a trade truce and the Iran war on the agenda.
item.content:encoded: President Trump meets with China's President Xi Jinping in Beijing with a trade truce and the Iran war on the agenda.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: 18 passengers return to U.S. after weeks on cruise ship hit with hantavirus outbreak
item.description: Eighteen U.S. passengers have returned stateside after weeks on the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak. They're being monitored at specialized medical facilities.
item.content:encoded: Eighteen U.S. passengers have returned stateside after weeks on the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak. They're being monitored at specialized medical facilities.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang
